title: Popular sugar substitutes linked to faster brain aging
excerpt: Several popular sugar substitutes may not be as harmless as they seem. Adults who consumed the most artificial sweeteners showed substantially faster declines in memory and thinking, especially if they were under 60 or had diabetes. The highest intake was linked to cognitive aging roughly 1.6 years faster than the lowest intake. Researchers stressed that more studies are needed before concluding that sweeteners are the cause.
content_html:
Several widely used sugar substitutes may be associated with faster declines in memory and thinking skills over time, according to research published in Neurology, the medical journal of the American Academy of Neurology.

The study followed nearly 13,000 adults and examined seven sweeteners that contain little or no calories. People who consumed the largest total amounts showed a more rapid decline in cognitive abilities than those who consumed the smallest amounts. The association was especially strong among people with diabetes.

The results do not show that sweeteners directly cause cognitive decline. They reveal an association, meaning other factors could help explain the pattern.

Seven Common Sweeteners Examined

Researchers studied aspartame, saccharin, acesulfame K, erythritol, xylitol, sorbitol and tagatose.

These ingredients are frequently added to ultra processed products, including flavored water, soft drinks, energy drinks, yogurt and desserts marketed as low in calories. Several are also sold separately for use in coffee, tea, cooking or baking.

"Low- and no-calorie sweeteners are often seen as a healthy alternative to sugar, however our findings suggest certain sweeteners may have negative effects on brain health over time," said study author Claudia Kimie Suemoto, MD, PhD, of the University of São Paulo in Brazil.

Tracking Brain Health for Eight Years

The research included 12,772 adults living across Brazil. Participants were 52 years old on average and were monitored for approximately eight years.

At the beginning of the study, participants completed detailed food questionnaires describing what they had eaten and drunk during the previous year. Researchers then placed them into three groups according to their total sweetener intake.

People in the lowest consumption group averaged 20 milligrams per day (mg/day), while those in the highest group averaged 191 mg/day. In the case of aspartame, the amount consumed by the highest group was roughly equal to the aspartame in one can of diet soda.

Sorbitol was consumed in the largest quantity of any individual sweetener, with an average daily intake of 64 mg/day.

Participants completed cognitive assessments at the beginning, midpoint and end of the study. The tests measured several aspects of brain function, including verbal fluency, working memory, word recall and processing speed.

Verbal fluency refers to the ability to quickly retrieve and produce words. Working memory is the brain's short term system for holding and using information, while processing speed reflects how quickly a person can understand and respond to information.

Higher Intake Linked to Faster Cognitive Decline

After accounting for age, sex, high blood pressure, cardiovascular disease and other relevant factors, the researchers found a clear difference between the intake groups.

People who consumed the greatest amounts of sweeteners experienced a 62% faster decline in overall thinking and memory abilities than those who consumed the least. Researchers estimated that this difference was comparable to about 1.6 additional years of aging.

Participants in the middle consumption group experienced a decline that was 35% faster than the decline observed in the lowest group. That difference was comparable to approximately 1.3 years of aging.

Stronger Association in Adults Under 60

Age appeared to influence the results. Among participants younger than 60, those who consumed the most sweeteners experienced faster declines in verbal fluency and overall cognitive performance than those who consumed the least.

Researchers did not find the same association among participants older than 60.

The connection between sweetener intake and faster cognitive decline was also stronger among people with diabetes than among those without the condition. People with diabetes may use sugar substitutes more frequently because they are often advised to limit products that rapidly raise blood sugar.

Six Sweeteners Linked to Memory Changes

When the researchers examined the sweeteners separately, six were associated with faster declines in overall cognition, particularly memory.

Those sweeteners were aspartame, saccharin, acesulfame K, erythritol, sorbitol and xylitol.

Tagatose was the only sweetener in the study that was not linked to cognitive decline.

"While we found links to cognitive decline for middle-aged people both with and without diabetes, people with diabetes are more likely to use artificial sweeteners as sugar substitutes," Suemoto said. "More research is needed to confirm our findings and to investigate if other refined sugar alternatives, such as applesauce, honey, maple syrup or coconut sugar, may be effective alternatives."

Important Limits of the Research

The study did not include every artificial sweetener currently used in food and beverages, so the findings cannot be applied to all sugar substitutes.

The dietary information was also provided by the participants themselves. Because people may forget foods or misjudge how much they consumed, self reported diet data can be imperfect.

Most importantly, the study was observational. It identified a relationship between higher sweetener consumption and faster cognitive decline, but it could not establish that the sweeteners caused those changes.

Key Findings

  • The study followed 12,772 adults with an average age of 52.
  • Researchers examined seven sweeteners commonly found in flavored water, soda, energy drinks, yogurt, low calorie desserts and other ultra processed foods.
  • Participants who consumed the largest total amounts experienced faster declines in overall thinking and memory skills than those who consumed the smallest amounts.
  • The difference was comparable to approximately 1.6 additional years of aging.
  • The association appeared in adults younger than 60 but was not detected in those older than 60.
  • The findings show a link, but they do not prove that sugar substitutes cause cognitive decline.

The research was supported by the Brazilian Ministry of Health, the Ministry of Science, Technology, and Innovation, and the National Council for Scientific and Technological Development.

sentences.zh: 根据美国神经病学学会医学杂志《神经病学》发表的研究，几种广泛使用的糖替代品可能与记忆力和思维能力随着时间的推移而更快下降有关。

People who consumed the largest total amounts showed a more rapid decline in cognitive abilities than those who consumed the smallest amounts. The association was especially strong among people with diabetes.

sentences.zh: 该研究跟踪了近 13,000 名成年人，并检查了七种含有很少或不含卡路里的甜味剂。

sentences.en: People who consumed the largest total amounts showed a more rapid decline in cognitive abilities than those who consumed the smallest amounts.
sentences.zh: 摄入总量最多的人比摄入最少的人认知能力下降得更快。

sentences.en: The association was especially strong among people with diabetes.
sentences.zh: 这种关联在糖尿病患者中尤其明显。

sentences.en: They reveal an association, meaning other factors could help explain the pattern.
sentences.zh: 它们揭示了一种关联，这意味着其他因素可以帮助解释这种模式。

blocks.html_en: Seven Common Sweeteners Examined

sentences.en: Seven Common Sweeteners Examined
sentences.zh: 检查七种常见甜味剂

blocks.html_en: Researchers studied aspartame, saccharin, acesulfame K, erythritol, xylitol, sorbitol and tagatose.

sentences.en: Researchers studied aspartame, saccharin, acesulfame K, erythritol, xylitol, sorbitol and tagatose.
sentences.zh: 研究人员研究了阿斯巴甜、糖精、安赛蜜、赤藓糖醇、木糖醇、山梨糖醇和塔格糖。

blocks.html_en: These ingredients are frequently added to ultra processed products, including flavored water, soft drinks, energy drinks, yogurt and desserts marketed as low in calories. Several are also sold separately for use in coffee, tea, cooking or baking.

sentences.en: These ingredients are frequently added to ultra processed products, including flavored water, soft drinks, energy drinks, yogurt and desserts marketed as low in calories.
sentences.zh: 这些成分经常被添加到超加工产品中，包括以低热量销售的调味水、软饮料、能量饮料、酸奶和甜点。

sentences.en: Several are also sold separately for use in coffee, tea, cooking or baking.
sentences.zh: 有几种还单独出售，用于咖啡、茶、烹饪或烘焙。

blocks.html_en: "Low- and no-calorie sweeteners are often seen as a healthy alternative to sugar, however our findings suggest certain sweeteners may have negative effects on brain health over time," said study author Claudia Kimie Suemoto, MD, PhD, of the University of São Paulo in Brazil.

sentences.en: "Low- and no-calorie sweeteners are often seen as a healthy alternative to sugar, however our findings suggest certain sweeteners may have negative effects on brain health over time," said study author Claudia Kimie Suemoto, MD, PhD, of the University of São Paulo in Brazil.
sentences.zh: “低热量和无热量甜味剂通常被视为糖的健康替代品，但我们的研究结果表明，随着时间的推移，某些甜味剂可能会对大脑健康产生负面影响，”该研究的作者、巴西圣保罗大学医学博士 Claudia Kimie Suemoto 说。

blocks.html_en: Tracking Brain Health for Eight Years

sentences.en: Tracking Brain Health for Eight Years
sentences.zh: 追踪大脑健康八年

blocks.html_en: The research included 12,772 adults living across Brazil. Participants were 52 years old on average and were monitored for approximately eight years.

sentences.en: The research included 12,772 adults living across Brazil.
sentences.zh: 该研究涵盖了居住在巴西各地的 12,772 名成年人。

sentences.en: Participants were 52 years old on average and were monitored for approximately eight years.
sentences.zh: 参与者平均年龄 52 岁，接受了大约八年的监测。

blocks.html_en: At the beginning of the study, participants completed detailed food questionnaires describing what they had eaten and drunk during the previous year. Researchers then placed them into three groups according to their total sweetener intake.

sentences.en: At the beginning of the study, participants completed detailed food questionnaires describing what they had eaten and drunk during the previous year.
sentences.zh: 在研究开始时，参与者完成了详细的食物调查问卷，描述他们在前一年吃过什么、喝过什么。

sentences.en: Researchers then placed them into three groups according to their total sweetener intake.
sentences.zh: 研究人员然后根据他们的甜味剂总摄入量将他们分为三组。

blocks.html_en: People in the lowest consumption group averaged 20 milligrams per day (mg/day), while those in the highest group averaged 191 mg/day. In the case of aspartame, the amount consumed by the highest group was roughly equal to the aspartame in one can of diet soda.

sentences.en: People in the lowest consumption group averaged 20 milligrams per day (mg/day), while those in the highest group averaged 191 mg/day.
sentences.zh: 最低摄入量组的人平均每天 20 毫克 (mg/day)，而最高摄入量组的人平均每天 191 毫克。

sentences.en: In the case of aspartame, the amount consumed by the highest group was roughly equal to the aspartame in one can of diet soda.
sentences.zh: 就阿斯巴甜而言，最高组的摄入量大致相当于一罐无糖汽水中的阿斯巴甜。

blocks.html_en: Sorbitol was consumed in the largest quantity of any individual sweetener, with an average daily intake of 64 mg/day.

sentences.en: Sorbitol was consumed in the largest quantity of any individual sweetener, with an average daily intake of 64 mg/day.
sentences.zh: 山梨醇是所有甜味剂中消耗量最大的，平均每日摄入量为 64 毫克/天。

blocks.html_en: Participants completed cognitive assessments at the beginning, midpoint and end of the study. The tests measured several aspects of brain function, including verbal fluency, working memory, word recall and processing speed.

sentences.en: Participants completed cognitive assessments at the beginning, midpoint and end of the study.
sentences.zh: 参与者在研究开始、中期和结束时完成了认知评估。

sentences.en: The tests measured several aspects of brain function, including verbal fluency, working memory, word recall and processing speed.
sentences.zh: 这些测试测量了大脑功能的几个方面，包括言语流畅性、工作记忆、单词回忆和处理速度。

blocks.html_en: Verbal fluency refers to the ability to quickly retrieve and produce words. Working memory is the brain's short term system for holding and using information, while processing speed reflects how quickly a person can understand and respond to information.

sentences.en: Verbal fluency refers to the ability to quickly retrieve and produce words.
sentences.zh: 言语流畅性是指快速检索和说出单词的能力。

sentences.en: Working memory is the brain's short term system for holding and using information, while processing speed reflects how quickly a person can understand and respond to information.
sentences.zh: 工作记忆是大脑保存和使用信息的短期系统，而处理速度则反映了一个人理解和响应信息的速度。

blocks.html_en: Higher Intake Linked to Faster Cognitive Decline

sentences.en: Higher Intake Linked to Faster Cognitive Decline
sentences.zh: 摄入量越高，认知能力下降速度越快

blocks.html_en: After accounting for age, sex, high blood pressure, cardiovascular disease and other relevant factors, the researchers found a clear difference between the intake groups.

sentences.en: After accounting for age, sex, high blood pressure, cardiovascular disease and other relevant factors, the researchers found a clear difference between the intake groups.

blocks.html_en: People who consumed the greatest amounts of sweeteners experienced a 62% faster decline in overall thinking and memory abilities than those who consumed the least. Researchers estimated that this difference was comparable to about 1.6 additional years of aging.

sentences.en: People who consumed the greatest amounts of sweeteners experienced a 62% faster decline in overall thinking and memory abilities than those who consumed the least.
sentences.zh: 摄入最多甜味剂的人的整体思维和记忆能力下降速度比摄入最少的人快 62%。

sentences.en: Researchers estimated that this difference was comparable to about 1.6 additional years of aging.
sentences.zh: 研究人员估计，这种差异相当于额外衰老约 1.6 年。

blocks.html_en: Participants in the middle consumption group experienced a decline that was 35% faster than the decline observed in the lowest group. That difference was comparable to approximately 1.3 years of aging.

sentences.en: Participants in the middle consumption group experienced a decline that was 35% faster than the decline observed in the lowest group.
sentences.zh: 中等消费群体的参与者的下降速度比最低消费群体的下降速度快 35%。

sentences.en: That difference was comparable to approximately 1.3 years of aging.
sentences.zh: 这一差异相当于衰老了大约 1.3 年。

blocks.html_en: Stronger Association in Adults Under 60

sentences.zh: 60 岁以下成年人的关联性更强

blocks.html_en: Age appeared to influence the results. Among participants younger than 60, those who consumed the most sweeteners experienced faster declines in verbal fluency and overall cognitive performance than those who consumed the least.

sentences.en: Age appeared to influence the results.
sentences.zh: 年龄似乎会影响结果。

sentences.en: Among participants younger than 60, those who consumed the most sweeteners experienced faster declines in verbal fluency and overall cognitive performance than those who consumed the least.
sentences.zh: 在 60 岁以下的参与者中，摄入甜味剂最多的人比摄入最少的人的语言流畅性和整体认知能力下降得更快。

blocks.html_en: Researchers did not find the same association among participants older than 60.

sentences.en: Researchers did not find the same association among participants older than 60.
sentences.zh: 研究人员没有在 60 岁以上的参与者中发现同样的关联。

blocks.html_en: The connection between sweetener intake and faster cognitive decline was also stronger among people with diabetes than among those without the condition. People with diabetes may use sugar substitutes more frequently because they are often advised to limit products that rapidly raise blood sugar.

sentences.en: The connection between sweetener intake and faster cognitive decline was also stronger among people with diabetes than among those without the condition.
sentences.zh: 与非糖尿病患者相比，糖尿病患者摄入甜味剂与认知能力下降速度更快之间的联系也更强。

sentences.en: People with diabetes may use sugar substitutes more frequently because they are often advised to limit products that rapidly raise blood sugar.
sentences.zh: 糖尿病患者可能会更频繁地使用糖替代品，因为他们经常被建议限制快速升高血糖的产品。

sentences.zh: 这种关联出现在 60 岁以下的成年人中，但在 60 岁以上的成年人中没有发现。

blocks.html_en: • The findings show a link, but they do not prove that sugar substitutes cause cognitive decline.

sentences.en: The findings show a link, but they do not prove that sugar substitutes cause cognitive decline.
sentences.zh: 研究结果显示了两者之间的联系，但并不能证明糖替代品会导致认知能力下降。

blocks.html_en: The research was supported by the Brazilian Ministry of Health, the Ministry of Science, Technology, and Innovation, and the National Council for Scientific and Technological Development.

sentences.en: The research was supported by the Brazilian Ministry of Health, the Ministry of Science, Technology, and Innovation, and the National Council for Scientific and Technological Development.
sentences.zh: 该研究得到了巴西卫生部、科学、技术和创新部以及国家科学技术发展委员会的支持。

content_html_zh:
根据美国神经病学学会医学杂志《神经病学》发表的研究，几种广泛使用的糖替代品可能与记忆力和思维能力随着时间的推移而更快下降有关。

该研究跟踪了近 13,000 名成年人，并检查了七种含有很少或不含卡路里的甜味剂。 摄入总量最多的人比摄入最少的人认知能力下降得更快。 这种关联在糖尿病患者中尤其明显。

结果并未表明甜味剂直接导致认知能力下降。 它们揭示了一种关联，这意味着其他因素可以帮助解释这种模式。

检查七种常见甜味剂

研究人员研究了阿斯巴甜、糖精、安赛蜜、赤藓糖醇、木糖醇、山梨糖醇和塔格糖。

这些成分经常被添加到超加工产品中，包括以低热量销售的调味水、软饮料、能量饮料、酸奶和甜点。 有几种还单独出售，用于咖啡、茶、烹饪或烘焙。

“低热量和无热量甜味剂通常被视为糖的健康替代品，但我们的研究结果表明，随着时间的推移，某些甜味剂可能会对大脑健康产生负面影响，”该研究的作者、巴西圣保罗大学医学博士 Claudia Kimie Suemoto 说。

追踪大脑健康八年

该研究涵盖了居住在巴西各地的 12,772 名成年人。 参与者平均年龄 52 岁，接受了大约八年的监测。

在研究开始时，参与者完成了详细的食物调查问卷，描述他们在前一年吃过什么、喝过什么。 研究人员然后根据他们的甜味剂总摄入量将他们分为三组。

最低摄入量组的人平均每天 20 毫克 (mg/day)，而最高摄入量组的人平均每天 191 毫克。 就阿斯巴甜而言，最高组的摄入量大致相当于一罐无糖汽水中的阿斯巴甜。

山梨醇是所有甜味剂中消耗量最大的，平均每日摄入量为 64 毫克/天。

参与者在研究开始、中期和结束时完成了认知评估。 这些测试测量了大脑功能的几个方面，包括言语流畅性、工作记忆、单词回忆和处理速度。

言语流畅性是指快速检索和说出单词的能力。 工作记忆是大脑保存和使用信息的短期系统，而处理速度则反映了一个人理解和响应信息的速度。

摄入量越高，认知能力下降速度越快

在考虑了年龄、性别、高血压、心血管疾病和其他相关因素后，研究人员发现摄入组之间存在明显差异。

摄入最多甜味剂的人的整体思维和记忆能力下降速度比摄入最少的人快 62%。 研究人员估计，这种差异相当于额外衰老约 1.6 年。

中等消费群体的参与者的下降速度比最低消费群体的下降速度快 35%。 这一差异相当于衰老了大约 1.3 年。

60 岁以下成年人的关联性更强

年龄似乎会影响结果。 在 60 岁以下的参与者中，摄入甜味剂最多的人比摄入最少的人的语言流畅性和整体认知能力下降得更快。

研究人员没有在 60 岁以上的参与者中发现同样的关联。

与非糖尿病患者相比，糖尿病患者摄入甜味剂与认知能力下降速度更快之间的联系也更强。 糖尿病患者可能会更频繁地使用糖替代品，因为他们经常被建议限制快速升高血糖的产品。

六种甜味剂与记忆变化有关

当研究人员分别检查甜味剂时，发现有六种甜味剂与整体认知（尤其是记忆力）的更快下降有关。

这些甜味剂包括阿斯巴甜、糖精、安赛蜜、赤藓糖醇、山梨糖醇和木糖醇。

塔格糖是该研究中唯一与认知能力下降无关的甜味剂。

“虽然我们发现无论是否患有糖尿病，中年人的认知能力都会下降，但糖尿病患者更有可能使用人工甜味剂作为糖替代品，”Suemoto 说。 “需要更多的研究来证实我们的发现，并调查其他精制糖替代品，如苹果酱、蜂蜜、枫糖浆或椰子糖，是否可能是有效的替代品。”

研究的重要局限性

该研究并未包括目前食品和饮料中使用的所有人造甜味剂，因此研究结果不能适用于所有糖替代品。

饮食信息也由参与者自己提供。 由于人们可能会忘记食物或误判自己消耗了多少食物，因此自我报告的饮食数据可能并不完美。

最重要的是，这项研究是观察性的。 它确定了较高的甜味剂消耗量与较快的认知能力下降之间的关系，但无法确定甜味剂导致了这些变化。

主要发现

  • 该研究跟踪了 12,772 名平均年龄为 52 岁的成年人。
  • 研究人员检查了调味水、苏打水、能量饮料、酸奶、低热量甜点和其他超加工食品中常见的七种甜味剂。
  • 消耗总量最多的参与者比消耗最少的参与者整体思维和记忆能力下降得更快。
  • 这一差异相当于额外衰老了约 1.6 年。
  • 这种关联出现在 60 岁以下的成年人中，但在 60 岁以上的成年人中没有发现。
  • 研究结果显示了两者之间的联系，但并不能证明糖替代品会导致认知能力下降。

该研究得到了巴西卫生部、科学、技术和创新部以及国家科学技术发展委员会的支持。
